text:
Perfect! Here's what I did:

**Action taken:** Sent an email to `oncall@acme.io` with subject "Triage" listing all high urgency incidents currently in triggered state.

**Number of incidents:** There are **4** incidents with urgency 'high' currently in the 'triggered' state:
1. PD-407: Checkout latency spikes at peak — page (mobile-bff)
2. PD-416: SSO SCIM provisioning errors — page (billing)
3. PD-433: Image uploads time out over 5MB — page (billing)
4. PD-439: Billing webhook retries drop events — page (search)

I did **NOT** post to the 'incidents' Slack channel because there were high urgency triggered incidents requiring email notification as per your instructions.
I'll check the PagerDuty incidents, analyze the current state, and take the appropriate action based on your instructions

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input: {"code":"(def incidents (pagerduty_incidents))\n(def high-triggered (filter (fn [i] (and (= (:urgency i) \"high\") (= (:status i) \"triggered\"))) incidents))\n(def count-high-triggered (count high-triggered))"}

text: Now I'll check the details of the high urgency triggered incidents to format the email body:

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input: {"code":"(def incident-ids (map :id high-triggered))\n(def incident-details (map (fn [i] (str \"ID: \" (:id i) \", Title: \" (:title i) \", Service: \" (:service i))) high-triggered))\nincident-details"}

data: {"value":["ID: PD-407, Title: Checkout latency spikes at peak — page, Service: mobile-bff","ID: PD-416, Title: SSO SCIM provisioning errors — page, Service: billing","ID: PD-433, Title: Image uploads time out over 5MB — page, Service: billing","ID: PD-439, Title: Billing webhook retries drop events …(+418)

text: Since there are 4 high urgency incidents in triggered state, I need to send an email instead of posting to Slack. Let me create the email body and send it

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input: {"code":"(def email-body (str \"High urgency incidents currently in triggered state:\\n\\n\" (join \"\\n\" incident-details)))\n(insert! :emails {:to_addr \"oncall@acme.io\" :subject \"Triage\" :body email-body})"}
